Name: Does God Exist — Hitchens vs Turek (VCU)
Description: Rulebook formalizing the VCU debate 'Does God Exist?' between Christopher Hitchens (atheism/anti-theism) and Frank Turek (theism), and every philosophical concept raised. Built via the Shadle steps: raw text -> vocabulary -> glossary -> narrative -> mock data -> normalized schema -> this DAG-structured hub.

Debaters.Description: The two principals and the moderator of the debate.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Logical primary key — slug of Label.

schema.name: Side
schema.Description: Role in the debate: affirmative, negative, or moderator.

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: Biographical note and stance. (Worldviews defended are the reverse of Worldviews.ChampionedBy.)

data.Label: Frank Turek
data.Description: Christian apologist, founder of CrossExamined.org, co-author of 'I Don't Have Enough Faith to Be an Atheist'. Argues the affirmative for a generic theism.

data.Label: Christopher Hitchens
data.Description: Author of 'God Is Not Great'. Argues the negative; self-describes as anti-theist, not merely atheist.

data.Label: Timothy Hulley
data.Description: Dean of the VCU Honors College; moderates phases and relays audience questions for the event hosted by the United Secular Alliance.

Worldviews.Description: Competing positions on God and reality contested in the debate.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Logical primary key — slug of Label.

schema.name: AffirmsGod
schema.Description: Whether the worldview affirms the existence of God.

schema.name: ChampionedBy
schema.Description: Debater who principally champions this worldview.

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: What the worldview holds in this debate's context.

data.Label: Theism
data.Description: A personal God who created and intervenes in the universe, answers prayer, and grounds morality.

data.Label: Deism
data.Description: A designer who set the cosmos in order but does not intervene or reveal itself; Hitchens treats it as separable from theism.

data.Label: Atheism
data.Description: No advanced argument for God has survived rebuttal; not the dogmatic claim no God could exist.

data.Label: Anti-theism
data.Description: Hitchens's stronger stance: relief that no celestial dictatorship exists, which he would oppose if it did.

data.Label: Agnosticism
data.Description: Suspension of judgment; embodied by Jastrow, cited by Turek as a hostile witness for the Big Bang.

data.Label: Materialism
data.Description: Only matter exists; mind, reason, and morality reduce to physical processes. Closely tied to naturalism; Turek's main target.

data.Label: Humanism
data.Description: Ethics grounded in innate solidarity, empathy, and conscience without divine sanction.

data.Label: Christianity
data.Description: The specific tradition Turek personally holds but brackets in favor of generic theism for the debate.

Arguments.Description: The named arguments advanced by each side, with derived development and contestation metrics.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Logical primary key — slug of Label.

schema.name: Conclusion
schema.Description: What the argument purports to establish.

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: Summary of the argument as presented.

schema.name: IsContested
schema.Description: 3rd-order: at least one supporting claim was rebutted.

data.Label: Cosmological Argument
data.Conclusion: The universe had a beginning, so it had a beginner.
data.Description: From the Big Bang: a beginning implies a beginner; SURGE supplies the evidence.

data.Label: Teleological Argument
data.Conclusion: Order and apparent purpose imply a designer.
data.Description: The design argument; contains fine-tuning and design-of-life as sub-arguments.

data.Label: Argument From Fine-Tuning
data.Description: Constants on a razor's edge (the gravitational force to 1 part in 10^40, expansion rate, axial tilt, Jupiter).

data.Label: Argument From Design Of Life
data.Conclusion: DNA's specified complexity implies a mind.
data.Description: Information only comes from minds; random mutation and macro-evolution cannot supply first life; panspermia concedes natural origin fails.

data.Label: Moral Argument
data.Conclusion: Objective moral values require a moral lawgiver.
data.Description: A prescription needs a prescriber; the standard is God's nature.

data.Label: Argument From Reason
data.Conclusion: Trustworthy reason cannot be mere chemistry.
data.Description: If thoughts are only chemical reactions, we cannot trust them (Darwin's doubt).

data.Label: Argument From Mathematics
data.Conclusion: Mathematical order comes from mind, not matter.
data.Description: Rationality and mathematics do not arise from randomness.

data.Label: Argument From Free Will
data.Conclusion: Real freedom is impossible under materialism.
data.Description: Molecules in motion (per Provine) afford no genuine human freedom.

data.Label: Argument From Consciousness
data.Description: Why is some carbon-based matter conscious? Materialism (Dennett) calls it an illusion.

data.Label: Argument From Poor Design
data.Conclusion: Waste and ruin suggest capricious or absent design.
data.Description: Extinction, collision courses, heat death — not the marks of a careful tuner.

data.Label: Deism-Theism Gap
data.Conclusion: A designer is not yet a prayer-answering God.
data.Description: Even granting design, the theist still owes virgin birth, resurrection, vicarious redemption.

data.Label: Morality Without God
data.Description: No moral act is unique to believers; some wicked acts are unique to them; conscience is innate.

data.Label: Religion Poisons Everything
data.Conclusion: Religion is a corrupting totalitarian tyranny.
data.Description: Its eschatology yearns for the world's end; it demands a master-slave submission.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Logical primary key — slug of Label.

schema.name: Ordinal
schema.Description: Position of the premise within its argument.

data.Label: Universe Began
data.Description: Premise 1 of the cosmological argument.

data.Label: Beginning Needs Beginner
data.Statement: Whatever begins to exist has a cause/beginner.
data.Description: Premise 2 of the cosmological argument.

data.Label: Cause Is Transcendent
data.Statement: The creator-cause is spaceless, timeless, immaterial, intelligent, powerful, and personal.
data.Description: Premise 3 of the cosmological argument.

data.Label: Fine-Tuning Observed
data.Statement: Physical constants are set on a razor's edge.
data.Description: Premise 1 of the argument from fine tuning.

data.Label: Chance Insufficient
data.Statement: Such precision is not plausibly due to chance.
data.Description: Premise 2 of the argument from fine tuning.

data.Label: Tuner Inferred
data.Statement: A fine-tuner best explains the balance.
data.Description: Premise 3 of the argument from fine tuning.

data.Label: Life Carries Information
data.Description: Premise 1 of the argument from design of life.

data.Label: Information From Minds
data.Description: Premise 2 of the argument from design of life.

data.Label: Designer Inferred
data.Statement: A mind best explains biological information.
data.Description: Premise 3 of the argument from design of life.

data.Label: Objective Values Exist
data.Statement: Objective moral values and duties exist.
data.Description: Premise 1 of the moral argument.

data.Label: Values Need Lawgiver
data.Statement: Objective values require a standard beyond humanity.
data.Description: Premise 2 of the moral argument.

data.Label: Lawgiver Is God
data.Statement: That standard is the moral lawgiver, God's nature.
data.Description: Premise 3 of the moral argument.

data.Label: Atrocity From Permission
data.Statement: The worst atrocities flow from felt divine permission.
data.Description: Premise 1 of the morality without god.

data.Label: No Unique Believer Virtue
data.Statement: No moral act is available only to believers.
data.Description: Premise 2 of the morality without god.

data.Label: Conscience Is Innate
data.Statement: Conscience (Socrates, Smith) is innate, not granted.
data.Description: Premise 3 of the morality without god.

data.Label: Designer Not Pray-Answerer
data.Statement: A designer need not answer prayer or perform miracles.
data.Description: Premise 1 of the deism theism gap.

data.Label: Burden Remains
data.Statement: The theist must still bridge from designer to revealed God.
data.Description: Premise 2 of the deism theism gap.

data.Label: Waste And Extinction
data.Statement: Profuse creation is mostly wiped out; 99.8% of species are extinct.
data.Description: Premise 1 of the argument from poor design.

data.Label: Capricious Or Absent
data.Statement: Heat death and collisions imply capricious or no design.
data.Description: Premise 2 of the argument from poor design.

Evidence.Description: Empirical and theoretical evidence items cited in support of arguments.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Logical primary key — slug of Label.

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: What the evidence is and who found it.

data.Label: SURGE: Second Law
data.Description: Second law of thermodynamics — the universe runs toward heat death, so it cannot be eternal.

data.Label: SURGE: Universe Expanding
data.Description: Hubble's 1929 discovery of expansion; reversed, it collapses to a singularity.

data.Label: SURGE: Radiation Echo
data.Description: Cosmic afterglow found by Penzias and Wilson; remnant heat of the Big Bang.

data.Label: SURGE: Galaxy Seeds
data.Description: COBE's one-part-in-100,000 temperature variations that let galaxies form.

data.Label: SURGE: Einstein Relativity
data.Description: General relativity makes space-time-matter co-relative, originating together.

data.Label: DNA Information
data.Description: DNA's information content, likened to a thousand encyclopedias (citing Dawkins).

data.Label: Red Shift Increasing
data.Description: Krauss: the Hubble red-light-shift rate is increasing; the universe dissipates at speed.

Concepts.Description: Every philosophical concept raised in the debate, with category and introducer.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Logical primary key — slug of Label.

schema.name: Category
schema.Description: cosmology, design, moral, theological, method, or epistemic.

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: Meaning of the concept in this debate.

data.Label: Big Bang
data.Description: The accepted origin of the universe; Turek accepts it and infers a 'banger'.

data.Label: Heat Death
data.Description: The far-future maximal-entropy state; used by both (Turek vs eternity, Hitchens for poor design).

data.Label: Singularity
data.Description: The point reversed expansion collapses to; Turek equates it with 'nothing'.

data.Label: Fine-Tuning
data.Description: Constants on a razor's edge permitting life.

data.Label: Specified Complexity
data.Description: Information in living systems Turek says only minds produce.

data.Label: Watchmaker
data.Description: Paley's image: design implies a designer.

data.Label: Panspermia
data.Description: Crick/Hoyle's seeded-life proposal; Turek calls it a backhanded concession.

data.Label: Objective Moral Values
data.Description: Mind-independent moral facts at the center of the moral exchange.

data.Label: Moral Lawgiver
data.Description: The prescriber Turek infers from the prescription of morality.

data.Label: Is-Ought Problem
data.Description: Hume's bar on deriving an 'ought' from an 'is'.

data.Label: Divine Permission
data.Description: Felt divine authorization Hitchens links to atrocity.

data.Label: Conscience
data.Description: Innate moral sense (Socrates's daemon, Smith's internal witness).

data.Label: Empathy
data.Description: Biologically grounded basis for morality raised in audience Q&A.

data.Label: Vicarious Redemption
data.Description: Salvation by another's suffering through human sacrifice; Hitchens's central moral objection.

data.Label: Scapegoating
data.Description: Casting sins onto a substitute; called primitive and barbaric.

data.Label: Miracle
data.Description: A suspension of natural order (the regular course of nature), per Hume.

data.Label: Virgin Birth
data.Description: Parthenogenesis: a miracle a design argument can never reach, even if a male-free pregnancy could be tracked.

data.Label: Resurrection
data.Description: Hitchens notes it was 'commonplace' in the gospels.

data.Label: Eschatology
data.Description: End-times doctrine; the rapture, Armageddon, the apocalypse, a 'cult of death' set against a promised new Heaven and new earth.

data.Label: Mind-Forged Manacles
data.Description: Blake's phrase for self-imposed religious tyranny (limbo, hell).

data.Label: Celestial Dictatorship
data.Description: Theism framed as totalitarian, master-slave submission — the oldest enemy of our moral autonomy.

data.Label: Materialism
data.Description: Only matter exists; Turek's target across the four supporting arguments.

data.Label: Free Will
data.Description: Genuine choice Turek says materialism cannot supply.

data.Label: Consciousness
data.Description: Subjective experience Turek says chemicals cannot explain.

data.Label: Occam's Razor
data.Description: Dispose of needless assumptions — Hitchens removes the prime mover.

data.Label: Hume On Miracles
data.Description: Error is always likelier than a suspension of nature in one's favor.

data.Label: Non-Overlapping Magisteria
data.Description: Gould's thesis that science and religion address separate domains.

data.Label: Burden Of Proof
data.Description: Who must establish the claim; both accept a share.

data.Label: Ontological Argument
data.Description: To conceive a thing is for it to be 'real in the mind' (parodied via limbo).

data.Label: Borrowing From Theism
data.Description: Turek's charge: Hitchens must 'sit in God's lap to slap his face'.

data.Label: Problem Of Evil
data.Description: Implicit in 'created sick, commanded to be well' — suffering counts against a good designer.

data.Label: Choice Argument
data.Description: Turek's claim that moving from non-existence to existence requires a choice, and only persons choose.

data.Label: Divine Command
data.Description: The divine-command standard: right and wrong fixed by God's nature and command.

data.Label: Naturalism
data.Description: Natural causes suffice to explain the world; every origin question now has a natural or sufficient explanation.

data.Label: Determinism
data.Description: Physical law fixes all events (Einstein, Provine's Darwinism); Turek says it undercuts trust in reasoning.

data.Label: Probability Argument
data.Description: Turek's cross-examination framing: not a proof of God but a cumulative case that God is the more probable explanation.

data.Label: Irreducible Complexity
data.Description: Raised in audience Q&A: whether such biological structures could have formed by chance.

data.Label: Prime Mover
data.Description: The first-cause designer Hitchens applies Occam's razor to remove — 'we don't need the prime mover at all'.

data.Label: Optimal Design
data.Description: Turek's closing: design need not be optimal; you can't call it suboptimal without knowing the designer's purpose (vs. Gould's panda's thumb).

data.Label: Event Horizon
data.Description: Hawking's 'lip of the black hole'; Hitchens uses it to show our God-talk is the vocabulary of our infancy.

data.Label: Andromeda Collision
data.Description: The Andromeda galaxy on a direct collision course — Hitchens's exhibit for capricious or absent design.

data.Label: Termination Of Pregnancy
data.Description: The abortion exchange: Turek presses Hitchens's book on the unborn child / fetus; embryology and early viability are invoked.

data.Label: Contraception
data.Description: Hitchens calls it nonsensical and immoral for the Church to equate contraception with abortion; cites the ectopic pregnancy as a humanist no-starter.

data.Label: Good Samaritan
data.Description: The Samaritan helped without religious instruction and predates Christianity — morality 'written on your heart'.

data.Label: Parable Of The Talents
data.Description: Turek's counter that Jesus did not simply preach 'take no thought for tomorrow'.

data.Label: Original Sin
data.Description: 'We were well in the beginning but then we messed up'; the Fall, which Hitchens parodies as 'created sick, commanded to be well'.

data.Label: Atonement
data.Description: Christ's sacrifice is retroactive to everyone before him, so God 'always had a witness' (creation, conscience, Christ).

data.Label: Theocracy
data.Description: Hitchens's post-9/11 cause: opposing theocracy and its messianic, end-times depredations internationally.

data.Label: Amalekites Genocide
data.Description: God's Old Testament injunction to Moses that too many Amalekite children were spared — Hitchens's exhibit of divinely-commanded atrocity, not equivalent to a hard medical decision.

data.Label: Suicide Bombing
data.Description: Wickedness Hitchens says is characteristically religious, alongside genital mutilation and circumcision dictated 'in God's name'.

data.Label: Enlightenment Values
data.Description: The post-religious values Turek says Hitchens wants all to adopt — which Turek reframes as 'the dictatorship of Christopher Hitchens'.

data.Label: Solipsism
data.Description: Religion's appeal to self-regard — wanting to be objects of a divine plan — likened to a quick peek at one's horoscope.

Claims.Description: Specific assertions made by each debater, with derived rebuttal status.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Logical primary key — slug of Label.

schema.name: IsRebutted
schema.Description: 2nd-order: the claim drew at least one rebuttal.

data.Label: Beginning Implies Beginner
data.Description: The Big Bang's beginning requires a transcendent beginner.

data.Label: Constants Are Fine-Tuned
data.Description: Life-permitting constants sit on a razor's edge, implying a tuner.

data.Label: Information Implies Mind
data.Description: DNA's specified complexity could only come from a mind.

data.Label: Objective Morality Needs God
data.Description: Objective moral values require a moral lawgiver beyond humanity.

data.Label: Materialism Defeats Reason
data.Description: If thought is mere chemistry, reasoning cannot be trusted.

data.Label: Consciousness Defies Chemistry
data.Description: No materialist account explains why matter is conscious.

data.Label: Hitchens Borrows Morality
data.Description: Hitchens must assume God's moral standard to argue against God.

data.Label: Design Need Not Be Optimal
data.Description: Suboptimal design is still design; you can't judge it without the designer's purpose.

data.Label: Everyone Is An Atheist
data.Description: Everyone disbelieves in thousands of gods; the theist must justify the one exception.

data.Label: Designer Is Not Enough
data.Description: Even a designer doesn't yield answered prayer, virgin birth, or resurrection.

data.Label: Atrocity From Divine Permission
data.Description: The most brutal acts come from believing one has divine permission.

data.Label: No Virtue Unique To Believers
data.Description: No moral act exists that only a believer could perform.

data.Label: Fine-Tuning Cuts Both Ways
data.Description: Accelerating dissipation and extinction suggest capricious or absent design.

data.Label: Miracles Fail Humes Test
data.Description: A reported miracle is always less likely than the witness being mistaken.

data.Label: Vicarious Redemption Is Immoral
data.Description: One may pay a debt but cannot assume another's moral responsibility.

data.Label: Religion Poisons Everything
data.Description: Religion demands servile submission and yearns for the end of the world.

data.Label: Abortion Is A Double Standard
data.Description: If God taking a life prematurely is an outrage, why is Hitchens taking one via abortion a moral right?

Rebuttals.Description: Direct responses aimed at specific claims.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Logical primary key — slug of Label.

data.Label: Cause Need Not Choose
data.Description: 'I don't have to know' the origin; positing a choosing being just relabels the mystery.

data.Label: Accelerating Expansion
data.Description: Krauss: the red-shift rate is increasing — the cosmos is flying apart, hardly careful tuning.

data.Label: Information Argument Is Faith
data.Description: The origin-of-life quotes are all statements of uncertainty, not evidence of a mind.

data.Label: Morality Predates Atoms
data.Description: The question of the good predates Lucretius, Democritus, and Epicurus and the atomic theory; it needs no deity.

data.Label: I Am A Primate And Still Reason
data.Description: Being a primate doesn't bar reasoning about heroism and self-sacrifice.

data.Label: Borrowing Charge Is False
data.Description: Conscience is innate (Socrates, Smith); no borrowing from theism is required.

data.Label: Optimality Objection Misfires
data.Description: Profuse extinction and a heat-death trajectory are not what design predicts.

data.Label: Religious People Aren't The Point
data.Description: Bad believers say nothing about whether God exists; everything poisons religion.

data.Label: Hume's Premise Is Wrong
data.Description: Hume's 'regular beats rare' rule would deny the Big Bang and one's own birth — singular events happen.

data.Label: Not All Pardons Are Immoral
data.Description: Hitchens clarifies: not all pardons — only assuming another's responsibility — are immoral.

data.Label: Nature Is The Greater Abortion Provider
data.Description: A false distinction: nature, not God, ends most pregnancies; the unborn has a real claim, but that is no moral equivalent to a commanded genocide.

Thinkers.Description: Authorities invoked by each side, including hostile witnesses.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Logical primary key — slug of Label.

schema.name: CitedAsHostileWitness
schema.Description: Cited against their own sympathies (e.g., an atheist conceding fine-tuning).

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: Why they appear in the debate.

data.Label: Edwin Hubble
data.Description: Discovered cosmic expansion (1929).

data.Label: Arno Penzias
data.Description: Co-discovered the radiation echo; quoted on a balanced, supernatural-seeming universe.

data.Label: Robert Wilson
data.Description: Co-discovered the cosmic afterglow at Bell Labs.

data.Label: George Smoot
data.Description: Led the COBE result; quoted comparing it to looking at God.

data.Label: Stephen Hawking
data.Description: Invoked by both; Hitchens warns against drafting him as a deist.

data.Label: Robert Jastrow
data.Description: Agnostic astronomer; Turek's hostile witness for an abrupt creation.

data.Label: Arthur Eddington
data.Description: Called the cosmic beginning 'frankly supernatural'.

data.Label: Albert Einstein
data.Description: General relativity; a determinist invoked on both sides.

data.Label: Aristotle
data.Description: Defined nothing as 'what rocks dream about'.

data.Label: Richard Dawkins
data.Description: Hostile witness on DNA's information content.

data.Label: Francis Crick
data.Description: Co-discoverer of DNA; floated panspermia.

data.Label: Fred Hoyle
data.Description: Coined 'Big Bang'; spoke of a superintellect monkeying with physics.

data.Label: Chandra Wickramasinghe
data.Description: Called primordial-soup belief 'an article of faith'.

data.Label: Antony Flew
data.Description: Prominent atheist turned deist over DNA evidence.

data.Label: Steven Weinberg
data.Description: Atheist physicist on the delicate balance required for life.

data.Label: William Provine
data.Description: Materialist Darwinist who denies free will.

data.Label: Daniel Dennett
data.Description: Materialist who treats consciousness as an illusion.

data.Label: Charles Darwin
data.Description: Natural selection; Hitchens's 'greater emancipator'.

data.Label: William Paley
data.Description: The watchmaker analogy: design implies a designer.

data.Label: Thomas Aquinas
data.Description: Source of the ontological move Hitchens parodies.

data.Label: Thomas Jefferson
data.Description: Author of Virginia's statute on religious freedom; a deist.

data.Label: David Hume
data.Description: On miracles and the is-ought problem.

data.Label: Socrates
data.Description: His inner daemon as a model of conscience.

data.Label: Adam Smith
data.Description: The 'internal witness' in Theory of Moral Sentiments.

data.Label: C. S. Lewis
data.Description: Attributed conscience to the divine; Hitchens says without improving on Smith.

data.Label: Lawrence Krauss
data.Description: On the increasing red-shift rate.

data.Label: Stephen Jay Gould
data.Description: Panda's thumb (poor design) and non-overlapping magisteria.

data.Label: Georges Lemaitre
data.Description: Catholic physicist who first proposed the Big Bang.

data.Label: H. L. Mencken
data.Description: Listed thousands of dead gods in Hitchens's anthology.

data.Label: Fyodor Dostoevsky
data.Description: Brothers Karamazov; his character Smerdyakov voices the 'everything is permitted' line.

data.Label: Blaise Pascal
data.Description: Addressed those 'so made that they cannot believe'.

data.Label: Lucretius
data.Description: Atomic theory predating the morality debate; cited with Democritus and Epicurus.

data.Label: Francis Collins
data.Description: Supervisor of the Human Genome Project; Hitchens's 'occasional debating enemy', invoked on the age of Homo sapiens.

data.Label: William Gladstone
data.Description: Scholar of Latin and Greek; cited as having shown Greek moral precepts as 'prefigurations of Christianity'.

data.Label: Omar Khayyam
data.Description: Persian poet whose quatrain Hitchens borrows to ask Turek 'what is your authority for saying you know something I don't?'.

Quotations.Description: Citations used by debaters, stored as paraphrased gists to respect copyright.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Logical primary key — slug of Label.

schema.name: Speaker
schema.Description: Thinker the quotation is attributed to.

data.Label: Smoot On Looking At God
data.Speaker: george-smoot
data.Gist: Paraphrase: a religious person might see the COBE result as a glimpse of the divine.
data.Description: Paraphrased citation used in the debate (not verbatim).

data.Label: Jastrow's Mountain Of Ignorance
data.Speaker: robert-jastrow
data.Gist: Paraphrase: the scientist scaling the peak finds theologians already seated there.
data.Description: Paraphrased citation used in the debate (not verbatim).

data.Label: Eddington On The Beginning
data.Speaker: arthur-eddington
data.Gist: Paraphrase: the cosmic beginning seems frankly supernatural.
data.Description: Paraphrased citation used in the debate (not verbatim).

data.Label: Hoyle's Superintellect
data.Speaker: fred-hoyle
data.Gist: Paraphrase: a superintellect appears to have arranged physics, chemistry, and biology.
data.Description: Paraphrased citation used in the debate (not verbatim).

data.Label: Wickramasinghe On Faith
data.Speaker: chandra-wickramasinghe
data.Gist: Paraphrase: belief in life from a primordial soup is an article of faith.
data.Description: Paraphrased citation used in the debate (not verbatim).

data.Label: Dostoevsky's Permission
data.Speaker: fyodor-dostoevsky
data.Gist: Paraphrase: without God, everything is permitted — voiced by the story's basest character.
data.Description: Paraphrased citation used in the debate (not verbatim).

data.Label: Hume On Probability
data.Speaker: david-hume
data.Gist: Paraphrase: weigh whether nature was suspended for you, or whether you simply erred.
data.Description: Paraphrased citation used in the debate (not verbatim).

data.Label: Mencken's Dead Gods
data.Speaker: h.-l.-mencken
data.Gist: Paraphrase: a long catalogue of gods once worshipped and now believed by no one.
data.Description: Paraphrased citation used in the debate (not verbatim).

data.Label: Khayyam On Authority
data.Speaker: omar-khayyam
data.Gist: Paraphrase: would God give a secret to a fanatic crew and deny it to me? — borrowed to press Turek on his authority.
data.Description: Paraphrased citation used in the debate (not verbatim).
